people.name: Priya Nair
people.nextMove: Send a short alumni-lane thank-you and one sincere question.
openLoops: Thank her for the career-fair conversation

met.how: Asked about transitioning from computer science into product management

people.origin: Met during a crowded career-fair walkaround. Priya made product work feel learnable by naming one small experiment instead of prescribing a career plan.
notes: Offered warm, specific advice about product internships
Prefers concrete questions over broad requests to pick her brain

people.name: Marcus Chen
people.nextMove: Share the prototype lesson learned, not a generic status update.
openLoops: Send the promised one-paragraph prototype reflection

met.how: Marcus stayed after his judging shift to ask about the roughest part of the demo

people.origin: Marcus became a project mentor after treating an unfinished demo as evidence worth discussing rather than something to hide.
notes: Responds well to honest build notes
Introduced a useful way to separate user evidence from feature enthusiasm

people.name: Jordan Okonkwo
people.nextMove: Bring the accessibility test idea to the next club build night.

met.how: Paired on a critique of a confusing campus-service form

people.origin: Jordan is both a classmate and the lead of a student design club. Those contexts overlap, but requests made in one should not automatically spill into the other.
notes: Cares about keyboard testing before visual polish
Last conversation ended with a concrete build-night idea

people.name: Sam Reyes
people.nextMove: Send the revised opening and ask which moment still feels emotionally flat.
openLoops: Return notes on Sam's audio sketch

met.how: Traded critiques after both choosing the same difficult prompt

people.origin: Sam is a generous craft collaborator who makes specific work better. The relationship is strongest when there is an artifact on the table.
notes: Direct critique is welcome when attached to a draft
Avoid turning every check-in into a project request

people.name: Avery Kim
people.nextMove: No task. Reconnect when there is a real story to share.

met.how: Families became friends after years of shared block gatherings

people.origin: Avery belongs to a long, quiet hometown relationship that does not need a productivity cadence to remain real.
notes: Pinned so recency never becomes guilt

people.name: Riley Booth
people.nextMove: Keep the next class check-in social unless Riley brings up the prototype.
openLoops: Decide whether the prototype needs another work session

people.origin: Riley is both a classmate and a collaborator. The garden keeps those lives visible so a friendly hallway hello does not become an accidental work meeting.
notes: Context boundary: campus conversation is not automatically project time
